cards.front: WLCs only support [...] LAG.
DHCP option [...] can be used to tell APs the IP address of their WLC.

In the context of WLCs:
Port = [phsyical/logical]
Interface = [phsyical/logical]
The WLC [...] port is used for out-of-band management.
The WLC service port must connect to a switch [access/trunk] port
WLC [...] ports are the standard network ports that connect to the switched network and are used for data traffic.
The WLC [...] port is used to connect to another WLC to form an HA pair.
CAPWAP tunnels are formed to/from the WLC's [...] interface.
The WLC [...] interface can be used to connect to and manage the standby WLC of an HA pair.
The WLC [...] interface is used when communicating with wireless clients to relay DHCP requests, perform web authentication, etc.
The WLC [...] interface is bound to the service port and used for out-of-band management.
WLC [...] interfaces are used to map WLANs to VLANs.
WPA+WPA2 is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
802.1X is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
Static WEP is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
Static WEP + 802.1X is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
CKIP is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
None + EAP Passthrough is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
What formats can a WLAN PSK be configured in?
WLAN PSKs in ASCII format must be at least [...] characters in length.
Web Authentication is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
Web Passthrough is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
Conditional Web Redirect is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
Splash Page Web Redirect is a Layer [...] security mechanism.
[...] is the default QoS setting of a WLAN.
WLAN Platinum QoS = [...]
WLAN Gold QoS = [...]
WLAN Silver QoS = [...]
WLAN Bronze QoS = [...]
WLAN [...] QoS = voice
WLAN [...] QoS = video
WLAN [...] QoS = best effort
WLAN [...] QoS = background
[...] ACLs can be used to limit management traffic destined for the WLC itself.
